# desec_tokens (Data Source)

Retrieves a list of all deSEC API tokens for the authenticated account. Token secret values are never included.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Retrieve all tokens in the account
data "desec_tokens" "all" {}

# Output all token names
output "token_names" {
  value = [for t in data.desec_tokens.all.tokens : t.name]
}

# Output names of all currently valid tokens
output "valid_token_names" {
  value = [for t in data.desec_tokens.all.tokens : t.name if t.is_valid]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `tokens` (Attributes List) List of all tokens in the account. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tokens))

<a id="nestedatt--tokens"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tokens`

Read-Only:

- `allowed_subnets` (List of String) List of IP addresses or CIDR subnets that may authenticate using this token.
- `auto_policy` (Boolean) Whether the token automatically creates a permissive scoping policy for each domain it creates.
- `created` (String) Timestamp of token creation in ISO 8601 format.
- `id` (String) The token's UUID.
- `is_valid` (Boolean) Whether the token is currently valid (not expired).
- `last_used` (String) Timestamp of when the token was last used, or `null` if it has never been used.
- `max_age` (String) Maximum token lifetime as a duration string, or `null` if no age limit is set.
- `max_unused_period` (String) Maximum allowed period of disuse, or `null` if no limit is set.
- `name` (String) Human-readable token name.
- `owner` (String) Email address of the deSEC account that owns this token.
- `perm_create_domain` (Boolean) Whether this token may create new domains.
- `perm_delete_domain` (Boolean) Whether this token may delete domains.
- `perm_manage_tokens` (Boolean) Whether this token may manage tokens.
